Number of Arms Included.evidence: The methods section states that patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to receive either darolutamide or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. This indicates two arms: one experimental (darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel) and one control (placebo + ADT + docetaxel).

Total Participants - N.evidence: The document states in the RESULTS section: 'Between November 2016 and June 2018, a total of 1306 patients underwent randomization at 286 centers in 23 countries. A total of 651 patients were assigned to receive darolutamide and 655 patients were assigned to receive placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' This number represents the total number of participants included in either the treatment or control arm of the clinical trial.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel; Placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The methods and results sections repeatedly state that patients were randomly assigned to receive either darolutamide or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. Table 1 and Table 2 also use the labels 'Darolutamide–ADT–Docetaxel' and 'Placebo–ADT–Docetaxel' for the two arms.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Darolutamide (600 mg [two 300-mg tablets] twice daily with food) in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy (a luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone [LHRH] agonist or antagonist, or orchiectomy) and six cycles of docetaxel (75 mg per square meter of body-surface area on day 1 and every 21 days), with prednisone or prednisolone administered at the investigator's discretion.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The regimen is described in the METHODS section: 'we randomly assigned patients ... to receive darolutamide (at a dose of 600 mg [two 300-mg tablets] twice daily) or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' Further details are provided under PATIENTS AND INTERVENTIONS: 'All the patients received androgen-deprivation therapy (a luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone [LHRH] agonist or an LHRH antagonist) or underwent orchiectomy within 12 weeks before randomization and received six cycles of docetaxel (75 mg per square meter of body-surface area on day 1 and every 21 days), with prednisone or prednisolone administered at the investigator's discretion, initiated within 6 weeks after randomization.'

Control Arm - N.evidence: The control arm in this trial is the Placebo–ADT–Docetaxel group. Table 1 (Patient Demographic and Clinical Characteristics at Baseline) and Table 2 (Secondary Efficacy End Points) both report 'Placebo–ADT–Docetaxel (N=654)' for the full analysis set. This is confirmed in the Results section: 'a total of 651 patients were assigned to receive darolutamide and 655 patients were assigned to receive placebo... Of these patients, 1305 patients (651 in the darolutamide group and 654 in the placebo group) were included in the full analysis set.' Thus, the total number of participants in the control arm is 654.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: The number of participants in each treatment arm is reported multiple times, including in the Results section ('The primary analysis involved 1306 patients (651 in the darolutamide group and 655 in the placebo group)') and in Table 1 ('Darolutamide—ADT—Docetaxel (N=651)', 'Placebo—ADT—Docetaxel (N=654)'). The full analysis set included 651 in the darolutamide arm and 654 in the placebo arm, as confirmed in Table 2 and the figure legends. The slight difference in the placebo arm (655 randomized, 654 analyzed) is explained by one patient who was randomized to placebo but received darolutamide and was included in the placebo group for the full analysis set.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The value is reported in the RESULTS section: 'At the data cutoff date for the primary analysis (October 25, 2021), the median treatment duration was longer in the darolutamide group (41.0 months) than in the placebo group (16.7 months)...'

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The value is reported in the RESULTS section: 'At the data cutoff date for the primary analysis (October 25, 2021), the median treatment duration was longer in the darolutamide group (41.0 months) than in the placebo group (16.7 months)...'

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: androgen-receptor inhibitor (darolutamide) plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The Methods section and multiple other locations (e.g., Abstract, Table 1, and throughout the Results and Discussion) state that patients in the first experimental arm received darolutamide (an androgen-receptor inhibitor) in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. This combination is consistently described as the treatment arm throughout the document.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document does not report results on the quality of life of participants. While it mentions assessments of pain and disease-related physical symptoms using validated questionnaires (Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form and Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate symptom index), the results for quality of life or these questionnaires are not presented in the results, tables, or figures. There is no mention of quality of life outcomes or data in the abstract, results, or discussion sections.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).value: 43.7 months in the darolutamide group and 42.4 months in the placebo group
Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The Results section states: 'The median follow-up for overall survival was 43.7 months in the darolutamide group and 42.4 months in the placebo group.' This is the median follow-up duration for the two arms of the whole study population.

NCT.evidence: The ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02799602, is explicitly stated in the abstract conclusions section: '(Funded by Bayer and Orion Pharma; ARASENS ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02799602.)'

PubMed ID.evidence: The document provides a DOI (10.1056/NEJMoa2119115) but does not list a PubMed ID anywhere in the text.

Trial Name.evidence: The trial is repeatedly referred to as the 'ARASENS' trial throughout the document, including in the title, author list, and methods section.

Author.evidence: The author list at the beginning of the article starts with 'Matthew R. Smith, M.D., Ph.D., Maha Hussain, M.D., Fred Saad, M.D., ... and Bertrand Tombal, M.D., Ph.D., for the ARASENS Trial Investigators', so 'Matthew R. Smith et al.' is appropriate.

Year.evidence: The article states 'This article was published on February 17, 2022, at NEJM.org.'

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a full publication from The New England Journal of Medicine, not just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: The trial is described as a 'phase 3 trial' in both the abstract and methods sections.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document describes the primary results of the ARASENS trial, not a follow-up or secondary analysis.

Control Arm.value: Placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Control Arm.evidence: The methods section states that patients were randomly assigned to receive darolutamide or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. The results and tables (e.g., Table 1, Table 2) consistently refer to the control arm as 'Placebo–ADT–Docetaxel.' The conclusion and discussion also describe the comparison as darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel versus placebo plus ADT and docetaxel.

Trial.value: ARASENS (ODM-201 in Addition to Standard ADT and Docetaxel in Metastatic Castration Sensitive Prostate Cancer) trial
Trial.evidence: The name 'ARASENS trial' is referenced multiple times in the document, including in the title ('Darolutamide and Survival in Metastatic, Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer'), in the author list ('for the ARASENS Trial Investigators'), and in the methods section ('In the phase 3 ARASENS (ODM-201 in Addition to Standard ADT and Docetaxel in Metastatic Castration Sensitive Prostate Cancer) trial, we evaluated the efficacy and safety of darolutamide added to androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel in patients with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.').

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (Patient Demographic and Clinical Characteristics at Baseline) reports 'Median age (range) — yr' for the Darolutamide—ADT—Docetaxel (N=651) group as 67 (41–89).

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Table 1 (Patient Demographic and Clinical Characteristics at Baseline) reports 'Median age (range) — yr' for the Placebo—ADT—Docetaxel (N=654) group as 67 (42–86).

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (Patient Demographic and Clinical Characteristics at Baseline) reports for the Darolutamide—ADT—Docetaxel (N=651) arm: ECOG performance-status score 0: 466 (71.6%).

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Table 1 (Patient Demographic and Clinical Characteristics at Baseline) reports for the Placebo—ADT—Docetaxel (N=654) arm: ECOG performance-status score 0: 462 (70.6%).

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 reports for the Darolutamide—ADT—Docetaxel (N=651) arm: ECOG performance-status score 1: 185 (28.4%). No patients with score 2 are reported, as eligibility was limited to 0 or 1.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Table 1 reports for the Placebo—ADT—Docetaxel (N=654) arm: ECOG performance-status score 1: 190 (29.1%). No patients with score 2 are reported, as eligibility was limited to 0 or 1.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: Table 1 (Patient Demographic and Clinical Characteristics at Baseline) reports 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis — no. (%)' for the Darolutamide—ADT—Docetaxel (N=651) group: 'M1, distant metastasis' is 558 (85.7%). This corresponds to synchronous metastases (metastatic at initial diagnosis) in the treatment arm.

Table 1 (Patient Demographic and Clinical Characteristics at Baseline) reports 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis — no. (%)' for the Placebo—ADT—Docetaxel (N=654) group: 'M1, distant metastasis' is 566 (86.5%). This corresponds to synchronous metastases (metastatic at initial diagnosis) in the control arm.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: Table 1 reports 'M0, no distant metastasis' at initial diagnosis for the Darolutamide—ADT—Docetaxel group as 86 (13.2%). These patients developed metastases after initial diagnosis (i.e., metachronous metastases) and are thus counted here.

Table 1 reports 'M0, no distant metastasis' at initial diagnosis for the Placebo—ADT—Docetaxel group as 82 (12.5%). These patients developed metastases after initial diagnosis (i.e., metachronous metastases) and are thus counted here.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The results are reported for the overall population with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. While Table 1 provides the percentage of patients with disease metastatic at initial diagnosis (synchronous, 86.1%), there is no reporting of outcomes (e.g., survival, secondary endpoints) stratified by synchronous vs. metachronous status. No tables, figures, or text present subgroup results by synchronous/metachronous groups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: Although Table 1 shows the proportion of patients with metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (synchronous) and, by implication, those with metachronous disease, the results (e.g., survival, secondary endpoints) are not reported separately for metachronous patients. No subgroup analyses or tables/figures present outcomes by metachronous status.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: There is no mention of high-volume or low-volume metastatic disease as prognostic subgroups in the results, tables, or figures. Subgroup analyses are mentioned (e.g., by metastasis stage: M1a, M1b, M1c), but not by high/low volume. No outcomes are reported by high-volume status.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: As with high volume, there is no reporting of results by low-volume metastatic disease. The document does not define or analyze outcomes by high/low volume subgroups, only by metastasis stage (M1a, M1b, M1c), which is not equivalent to high/low volume. No tables, figures, or text present outcomes by low-volume status.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: The secondary end points were time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to a first symptomatic skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms, time to initiation of opioid treatment for 7 or more consecutive days, and safety.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: This list is explicitly stated in the 'END POINTS' section: 'The secondary end points were time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to a first symptomatic skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms, time to initiation of opioid treatment for 7 or more consecutive days, and safety.'

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival, defined as the time from randomization until death from any cause.
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The Methods section under 'END POINTS' states: 'The primary end point was overall survival, which was defined as the time from randomization until death from any cause.' This is confirmed in the Abstract and Results sections, which repeatedly refer to overall survival as the primary endpoint.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: In the Methods and Results sections, it is stated that all patients in the darolutamide group (N=651) received androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. Table 1 and the trial design confirm that docetaxel was a required component of therapy for all randomized patients. Therefore, 100% of the treatment arm received docetaxel.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: In the Methods and Results sections, it is stated that all patients in the placebo group (N=654 in the full analysis set) received androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. Table 1 and the trial design confirm that docetaxel was a required component of therapy for all randomized patients. Therefore, 100% of the control arm received docetaxel.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Table 3 (Adverse Events) reports for Darolutamide-ADT-Docetaxel (N=652): Grade 3: 248 (38.0%), Grade 4: 183 (28.1%), Grade 5: 27 (4.1%). These are all-cause, worst grade adverse events. The sum of Grade 3, 4, and 5 is 458 (70.2%), but the table presents them as mutually exclusive worst grade per patient, so the percentages are not additive. The correct reporting is to list each grade as shown.

Table 3 (Adverse Events) reports for Placebo-ADT-Docetaxel (N=650): Grade 3: 232 (35.7%), Grade 4: 181 (27.8%), Grade 5: 26 (4.0%). These are all-cause, worst grade adverse events. The sum is not additive; each percentage is for patients whose worst event was that grade.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the RESULTS section: 'The overall survival at 4 years was 62.7% (95% CI, 58.7 to 66.7) in the darolutamide group.' This is the experimental arm (darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel).

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the RESULTS section: 'The overall survival at 4 years was ... 50.4% (95% CI, 46.3 to 54.6) in the placebo group.' This is the control arm (placebo + ADT + docetaxel).

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: Hazard ratio for time to castration-resistant prostate cancer (PFS proxy): 0.36 (95% CI, 0.30–0.42); Median not reached (NR)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 and Figure 2 (Panel A) report 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' as a key secondary endpoint, which serves as a proxy for PFS in this context. The hazard ratio for disease progression (darolutamide vs placebo) is 0.36 (95% CI, 0.30–0.42), with median PFS not reached (NR) in the darolutamide group. No explicit PFS rate (%) is reported, but the hazard ratio and median are provided for the overall population.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: Hazard ratio for time to castration-resistant prostate cancer (PFS proxy): 1 (reference); Median 19.1 months (95% CI, 16.5–21.8)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table 2 and Figure 2 (Panel A) report 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' for the placebo group (control). The hazard ratio is 1 (reference), and the median time to castration-resistant prostate cancer is 19.1 months (95% CI, 16.5–21.8). No explicit PFS rate (%) is reported, but the median and hazard ratio are provided for the overall population.

Treatment Class.value: androgen-receptor inhibitor (darolutamide) plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Treatment Class.evidence: The Methods section states that patients were randomly assigned to receive darolutamide or placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. The Background and Discussion sections further clarify that darolutamide is a potent androgen-receptor inhibitor. Thus, the treatment class is 'androgen-receptor inhibitor (darolutamide) plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'.

Type of Therapy.value: darolutamide (600 mg twice daily) or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel (six cycles of 75 mg/m2 every 21 days, with prednisone or prednisolone at investigator's discretion)
Type of Therapy.evidence: The Methods section states: 'we randomly assigned patients ... to receive darolutamide (at a dose of 600 mg [two 300-mg tablets] twice daily) or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' Further details in 'Patients and Interventions' specify all patients received androgen-deprivation therapy (LHRH agonist or antagonist or orchiectomy) and six cycles of docetaxel (75 mg/m2 every 21 days, with prednisone or prednisolone at investigator's discretion).

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document provides detailed information on trial design, randomization, blinding, statistical analysis, and results for efficacy and safety, but it does not explicitly report an overall risk judgment (such as 'low', 'moderate', or 'high' risk of bias) for individual criteria of evidence (COE) in the randomized controlled trial. There is no summary table or section with an overall risk judgment for the RCT according to standard risk of bias or COE frameworks.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The document describes a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial in which patients with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were assigned to receive darolutamide or placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. The primary comparison is between darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel versus placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. Thus, darolutamide is the add-on treatment given in combination with the primary treatments (androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel).

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Figure 1 (Kaplan–Meier curve) and its caption report 'Median Survival (95% CI) mo Darolutamide NE', where NE means 'not estimable'. This is the value for the experimental (darolutamide) arm for overall survival.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Figure 1 (Kaplan–Meier curve) and its caption report 'Median Survival (95% CI) mo Placebo 48.9 (44.4–NE)', which is the value for the control (placebo) arm for overall survival.
